title: Primary Event Criteria
description: The primary event defines the index date — the anchor point from which all inclusion/exclusion windows and exit strategies are measured. Every patient in the cohort must have at least one occurrence of the primary event during the observation period.
tips: Set 'Occurrence limit' to 'First' to study new-user / incidence populations.
Use 'All Events' for prevalence studies where repeated occurrences matter.
The observation window (Prior Days / Post Days) defines how much CDM history must exist around the event.
Multiple domain criteria within one primary event are OR-ed — any one qualifying event anchors the index date.
